fields.description:
## Why
Two defect classes concern the INVISIBLE AFFECTED SET (existing sites a plan silently breaks). A lexical trigger over-fires on descriptive prose; LLM routing is more reliable, and each overlay runs as its own criterion call (its own context window) -- preserving the intent behind the earlier "router->overlay" framing.

## What (REDESIGNED per review to be codebase-native)
The earlier "G1G2 emits an OVERLAY TRIGGER that orchestrator.py spawns" framing is dropped: verified that G1G2 produces findings (list[dict]), not a typed signal; route_criteria (orchestrator.py:332) routes overlays by criterion metadata, and the parallel Pass-1 fan-out has no G1G2->overlay ordering hook. Codebase-native path instead:
- Register TWO new LLM-routed overlay criteria (overlay_routing: llm, matching T10/T11) handled by the EXISTING route_criteria path -- zero new inter-pass signal:
  - G-5 prohibition-enumeration overlay: triggers on a plan introducing a NEW prohibition (enforce/block/require-before/must-pass); enumerates the outlawed behavior's existing call sites and classifies each MIGRATED / EXEMPTED / UNCOVERED (define this enum in the prompt).
  - G-10 CI-trigger/release-infra overlay: triggers on a new git ref / event source / schedule; enumerates every workflow trigger filter as INCLUDED / EXCLUDED / NO_FILTER (define enum) + release-infra coverage. Abstain-with-coverage on unparseable CI (polyglot fail-open).
- Each overlay is LLM-routed (reliable, not lexical) and runs as its own criterion call (the "own context window" the intent wanted). Enumerate consumers of criteria_routing.json schema before adding.

## What (edit targets)
criteria_routing.json (two new overlay criteria, overlay_routing: llm); new plan_review_G5_prohibition.md + plan_review_G10_citrigger.md prompts; orchestrator.py route_criteria only if a new overlay_routing value is needed (prefer reusing T10/T11's existing llm overlay path).

## Provenance
Gap-report labels (G-*/FP-*/R-*/WS-N) are tags from ~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md + session log brief-bride-rash (95f1-1b3b-a867-4020), NOT codebase symbols. Symbols introduced by a SIBLING story (e.g. committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim from WS1) are target-state, not pre-existing; the dependency is recorded.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Two overlay criteria registered (overlay_routing: llm) + their prompts. Proof: `python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.registry validate-routing` exits 0.
- [ ] MIGRATED/EXEMPTED/UNCOVERED and INCLUDED/EXCLUDED/NO_FILTER enums DEFINED in their prompts.
- [ ] G-5 recall case: a require-tests-before-merge plan yields UNCOVERED `gh pr merge` call sites. Proof: author `test_g5_prohibition` in tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py; `pytest -k g5_prohibition` green.
- [ ] G-10 recall case: a new ref pattern yields an EXCLUDED workflow; plus a fail-open abstain case on unknown CI. Proof: `pytest -k g10_citrigger` green.
- [ ] False-positive case: a plan that merely DESCRIBES enforcement (no new prohibition) does not fire. Proof: false-fire case in the eval suite.
